text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: this library's root component figures out whether the page/container it is rendered into uses right-to-left (RTL) text direction, and uses that to decide things like which arrow key means 'next slide'. It detects the direction by reading the computed CSS `direction` of its own root element (via `getComputedStyle`). The problem is that this detection only ever runs once, at the moment the lightbox's root element is first attached to the DOM — the result is then cached in component state for the lifetime of that mount and never rechecked. So if whatever controls the computed direction changes later — for example an ancestor element's `dir` attribute is toggled from `ltr` to `rtl` at runtime, after the lightbox has already mounted — the lightbox does not notice. It keeps behaving as if the direction were still whatever it detected at mount time (e.g. arrow keys keep working in the old direction's sense) instead of picking up the new, current direction.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the root direction state and its existing tests, then patch the library component and run the narrow Vitest coverage plus any relevant broader checks.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg -n \"direction|getComputedStyle|rtl|RTL\" src test* . --glob '"'!node_modules'"' --glob '"'!dist'"' | head -240 && rg --files | head -80"
/bin/bash -lc "cat package.json && rg -n \"MutationObserver|ResizeObserver|requestAnimationFrame|useEffect\\(\" src/components src/hooks src/modules | head -160"

text: The stale value is localized to `LightboxRoot`: its callback ref computes RTL only on attachment. I’m checking repository conventions and history now so the fix matches the project’s supported browser/runtime assumptions, then I’ll add a regression test that toggles an ancestor’s `dir` after mount.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "git log --all --oneline -- src/components/LightboxRoot.tsx | head -30 && git branch -a && sed -n '1,130p' src/modules/Portal.tsx && sed -n '1,100p' src/hooks/useContainerRect.ts && sed -n '1,180p' test/unit/test-utils.tsx"

text: The project already uses observer-based DOM synchronization for container measurements, and `MutationObserver` is available in the supported browser baseline. I’ll make RTL detection subscribe to relevant ancestor/document attribute changes (`dir`, `class`, and `style`), while retaining the immediate initial computation; this also covers direction changes expressed through CSS classes or inline styles.

The source change is in place. I’m adding a focused regression case in the existing RTL suite so the behavior is explicit: mount inline under LTR, flip the ancestor to RTL, then verify the keyboard mapping changes without remounting.

The focused suite passes all 11 RTL tests, including the new post-mount toggle case. I’m doing the final static checks and reviewing the diff for lifecycle details, especially observer cleanup on unmount.

text:
Fixed RTL detection in [LightboxRoot.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/components/LightboxRoot.tsx:19) by rechecking computed direction through a `MutationObserver` when relevant ancestor attributes change.

Added a regression test covering runtime `dir` changes in [RTL.spec.ts](/home/user/workspace/test/unit/RTL.spec.ts:94).

Verified:

- 11 RTL tests pass
- ESLint passes
- `git diff --check` passes
- TypeScript check remains blocked by existing `test/types` module-resolution errors
